name: python3-dev
title: python3-dev
summary: header files and a static library for Python (default)
description:
header files and a static library for Python (default)
Header files, a static library and development tools for building
Python modules, extending the Python interpreter or embedding Python
in applications.
.
This package is a dependency package, which depends on Debian's default
Python 3 version's headers (currently v3.13).

commands.name: python3-config
commands.description: output build options for python C/C++ extensions or embedding

arguments.description: print the the ABI flags as specified by PEP 3149.
arguments.raw: --abiflags  print the the ABI flags as specified by PEP 3149.

arguments.description: print the C compiler flags.
output build options for python C/C++ extensions or embedding
arguments.raw: -config  output build options for python C/C++ extensions or embedding

arguments.description: print the prefix used for executable program directories (such as bin, sbin, etc).
arguments.raw: --exec-prefix  print the prefix used for executable program directories (such as bin, sbin, etc).

arguments.description: print the extension suffix used for binary extensions.
arguments.raw: --extension-suffix  print the extension suffix used for binary extensions.

arguments.description: print the usage message.
similar to --cflags but only with -I options (path to python header files).
arguments.raw: --includes  similar to --cflags but only with -I options (path to python header files).

arguments.description: flags ] [ --extension-suffix ] [ --abiflags ] [ --help ]
print the flags that should be passed to the linker.
arguments.raw: --ldflags  print the flags that should be passed to the linker.

arguments.description: similar to --ldflags but only with -l options (used libraries).
arguments.raw: --libs  similar to --ldflags but only with -l options (used libraries).

arguments.description: prints the prefix (base directory) under which python can be found.
arguments.raw: --prefix  prints the prefix (base directory) under which python can be found.

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use python3-config because header files and a static library for Python (default).
When the engagement is in the install phase, use python3-config as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair python3-config with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `python3-config --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

arguments.name: --extension-suffix
arguments.description: print the extension suffix used for binary extensions.

arguments.name: --help

arguments.description: similar to --cflags but only with -I options (path to python header files).

arguments.name: --ld

arguments.description: print the flags that should be passed to the linker.
